rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory section and again in the discussion to support a specific mechanism-level analogy about immersion: deep engagement can steer subsequent choices toward similar activities. In the citing paper, this supports the argument that flow in gamified app use can make users less responsive to value-added tasks that interrupt gameplay. This is more than a background list citation because it helps justify and interpret a core moderating effect in the model.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Woolley and Sharif (2022) as conceptual support that immersion in one category can bias subsequent behavior toward similar engagement. It uses that logic to motivate and interpret its finding that flow during game engagement weakens conversion into value-added engagement.
